name: ISO 22301:2019
description: International standard for business continuity management systems (BCMS). Specifies requirements for planning, establishing, implementing, operating, monitoring, reviewing, maintaining, and continually improving a documented management system to protect against, reduce the likelihood of, and ensure recovery from disruptive incidents.
license_note: Metadata only. No ISO copyrighted text reproduced. Clause descriptions are original summaries.

items.title: Understanding the organization and its context
items.description: Requires determining external and internal issues relevant to the organization's purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcomes of its BCMS.
items.purpose: Establish the context for the business continuity management system.

items.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
items.description: Requires identification of interested parties relevant to the BCMS including customers, regulators, employees, and shareholders, and their requirements for business continuity.
items.purpose: Identify stakeholders and their business continuity requirements.

items.title: Determining the scope of the BCMS
items.description: Requires determining the boundaries and applicability of the BCMS, considering the organization's context, stakeholder requirements, products, services, and activities.
items.purpose: Define the scope and boundaries of the business continuity management system.

items.title: Business continuity management system
items.description: Requires establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving a BCMS including the processes needed and their interactions.
items.purpose: Establish and maintain the BCMS with required processes and interactions.

items.title: Leadership and commitment
items.description: Requires top management to demonstrate leadership and commitment by ensuring BCMS policy and objectives are established, resources are available, and the system achieves its intended outcomes.
items.purpose: Ensure top management demonstrates leadership and commitment to business continuity.

items.title: Policy
items.description: Requires establishing a business continuity policy appropriate to the organization's purpose, providing a framework for setting objectives, and including commitments to satisfy requirements and continual improvement.
items.purpose: Establish the business continuity policy as a framework for BCMS objectives.

items.title: Organizational roles, responsibilities and authorities
items.description: Requires assigning and communicating responsibilities and authorities for relevant roles within the BCMS, ensuring accountability for BCMS performance.
items.purpose: Assign and communicate roles and responsibilities for business continuity.

items.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities
items.description: Requires determining risks and opportunities that need to be addressed to ensure the BCMS achieves its intended outcomes and prevents undesired effects.
items.purpose: Address risks and opportunities affecting BCMS outcomes.

items.title: Business continuity objectives and plans to achieve them
items.description: Requires establishing measurable business continuity objectives at relevant functions and levels, consistent with the policy, and planning how to achieve them.
items.purpose: Establish measurable business continuity objectives and plan their achievement.

items.title: Planning changes to the BCMS
items.description: Requires that when changes to the BCMS are needed, they are carried out in a planned manner considering the purpose and potential consequences of the changes.
items.purpose: Ensure changes to the BCMS are planned and controlled.

items.title: Resources
items.description: Requires determining and providing resources needed for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving the BCMS.
items.purpose: Provide adequate resources for the business continuity management system.

items.title: Competence
items.description: Requires determining necessary competence of persons working under the organization's control that affects BCMS performance, and ensuring these persons are competent through education, training, or experience.
items.purpose: Ensure staff competence for business continuity activities.

items.title: Awareness
items.description: Requires persons working under the organization's control to be aware of the BC policy, their roles during disruptive incidents, and the implications of not conforming with BCMS requirements.
items.purpose: Ensure awareness of business continuity policy and individual roles during incidents.

items.title: Communication
items.description: Requires determining internal and external communications relevant to the BCMS including what, when, with whom, and how to communicate, including during disruptive incidents.
items.purpose: Establish communication processes for normal operations and during disruptive incidents.

items.title: Documented information
items.description: Requires maintaining documented information for the BCMS including policies, procedures, plans, and records. Covers creation, updating, and control of documents.
items.purpose: Maintain documented information required for BCMS effectiveness.

items.title: Operational planning and control
items.description: Requires planning, implementing, and controlling processes needed to meet BCMS requirements and to implement actions for managing risks and opportunities.
items.purpose: Plan and control operational processes for business continuity.

items.title: Business impact analysis and risk assessment
items.description: Requires conducting business impact analysis to identify prioritized activities and their resource requirements, and risk assessment to identify risks of disruption to those prioritized activities. Determines maximum tolerable period of disruption and recovery time objectives.
items.purpose: Identify critical activities and assess risks through business impact analysis and risk assessment.
kpis: RTO/RPO defined for all critical services

items.title: Business continuity strategies and solutions
items.description: Requires identifying and selecting business continuity strategies and solutions based on BIA and risk assessment outputs. Strategies address protection and stabilization of prioritized activities, management of impacts, and resource requirements.
items.purpose: Identify and select strategies and solutions to ensure continuity of prioritized activities.

items.title: Business continuity plans and procedures
items.description: Requires establishing and maintaining business continuity plans and procedures that provide a framework for managing disruptions. Plans cover incident response, business recovery, and restoration activities with clear roles, escalation paths, and communication procedures.
items.purpose: Establish plans and procedures for responding to and recovering from disruptive incidents.
kpis: BC plan coverage for critical activities

items.title: Exercise programme
items.description: Requires implementing and maintaining an exercise programme to validate business continuity plans and procedures. Exercises must be conducted at planned intervals, be consistent with BCMS scope, and produce post-exercise reports including outcomes and recommended improvements.
items.purpose: Validate business continuity plans through regular exercises and testing.

items.title: Evaluation of business continuity documentation and capabilities
items.description: Requires evaluating business continuity documentation, procedures, and capabilities through exercises, analysis of incidents, performance evaluation, and stakeholder feedback.
items.purpose: Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of BC documentation and capabilities.

items.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
items.description: Requires determining what needs to be monitored and measured, the methods used, when results shall be analyzed and evaluated, and who shall perform these activities.
items.purpose: Monitor and measure BCMS performance for evaluation and improvement.

items.title: Internal audit
items.description: Requires conducting internal audits at planned intervals to determine whether the BCMS conforms to the standard's requirements and the organization's own requirements, and is effectively implemented and maintained.
items.purpose: Verify BCMS conformance and effectiveness through internal audits.

items.title: Management review
items.description: Requires top management to review the BCMS at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness. Reviews consider status of actions from previous reviews, changes in context, performance data, and improvement opportunities.
items.purpose: Ensure continuing suitability of the BCMS through top management review.

items.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
items.description: Requires reacting to nonconformities, evaluating the need for action to eliminate causes, implementing corrective actions, and reviewing the effectiveness of corrective actions taken.
items.purpose: Address nonconformities and prevent recurrence through corrective action.

items.title: Continual improvement
items.description: Requires continually improving the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the BCMS through use of policy, objectives, audit results, analysis of monitored events, corrective actions, and management review.
items.purpose: Continually improve the business continuity management system.
